title: Error: Failed to copy content into new volume mount "some-hashed-container-code": cannot chown `~/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes/some-hashed-container-code/_data` : operation not permitted
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
While trying to run rootless podman gitlab container on ESXi virtual machine RHEL8 on SAN storage as non-root user, I got an error saying that chown operation is not permitted. If I change to root, then everything works fine.


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

0. First i had an error while trying to ```podman load -i gitlab-ee.tar``` (extract image from .tar file) saying that 
```
ERR[0047] Error pulling image ref gitlab-ee.tar: Error committing the finished image: error adding layer with blob "sha256:some-hashed-code": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): lchown /etc/gshadow: operation not permitted
```

1. As i'm using SAN storage, I changed Graphroot as [follows](https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/rootless-podman-nfs)
    * Changed ```graphroot``` in ~/.config/containers/storage.conf to /var/tmp/myuser/containers/storage
    * Then pulling image succeeded.

2. After checking the image on my local registry, ```podman run gitlab-ee``` reproduce the error.


**Describe the results you received:**
```
Error: Failed to copy content into new volume mount "some-hashed-container-code": cannot chown `/storage/home/myuser/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes/some-hashed-container-code/_data`: chown /storage/home/myuser/.local/share/containers/volumes/some-hashed-container-code/_data: operation not permitted
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Gitlab container runs without problem

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version: 2.0.5
API Version: 1
Go Version: go1.14.7
Built: Fri Sep 25 02:58:13 2020
OS/Arch: linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
host:
  BuildaVersion: 1.15.0
  Conmon:
    package: conmon-2.0.20-2.module+el8.3.0+475+893c1ab8.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.20, commit: 1019ecdeda3936be22162bb1cca308192145de53'
  Distribution:
    distribution: '"rhel"'
    version: "8.1"
  MemFree: 11191136256
  MemTotal: 33532796928
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-60.rc8.module+el8.1.0+4081+b29780af.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  SwapFree: 17179865088
  SwapTotal: 17179865088
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 4
  hostname: myuser
  kernel: 4.18.0-147.el8.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: true
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search:
  - registry.redhat.io
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - quay.io
  - docker.io
store:
  ConfigFile: /home/myuser/.config/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 0
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
  -  overlay.mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs
  GraphRoot: /var/tmp/myuser/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 1
  RunRoot: /run/user/4603
  VolumePath: /storage/home/myuser/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-2.0.5-5.module+el8.3.0+512+893clab8.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes, Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
RHEL 8.1 on ESXi Virtual machine 
```
$ whereis newuidmap
newuidmap: /usr/bin/newuidmap /usr/share/man/man1/newuidmap.1.gz
$ whereis newgidmap
newgidgidmap: /usr/bin/newgidmap /usr/share/man/man1/newgidmap.1.gz
$ whereis slirp4netns
slirp4netns: /usr/bin/slirp4netns /usr/share/man/man1/slirp4netns.1.gz
$ whereis fuse-overlayfs
fuse-overlayfs: /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs /usr/share/man/man1/fuse-overlayfs.1.gz

$ cat ~/.config/containers/storage.conf
[storage]
  drive = "overlay"
  runroot = "/run/user/4603"
  graphroot = "/var/tmp/myuser/containers/storage"
  [storage.options]
    size = ""
    remap-uids = ""
    remap-gids = ""
    remap-user = ""
    remap-group = ""
    ostree_repo = ""
    skip_mount_home = ""
    mount_program = "/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs"
    mountopt = ""
    [storage.options.thinpool]
      autoextend_precent = ""
      autoextend_threshold = ""
      basesize = ""
      blocksize = ""
      directlvm_device = ""
      directlvm_device_force = ""
      fs = ""
      log_level = ""
      min_free_space = ""
      mkfsarg = ""
      mountopt = ""
      use_deferred_deletion = ""
      use_deferred_removal = ""
      xfs_nospace_max_retries = ""

$ cat ~/.config/containers/libpod.conf
volume_path = "/storage/home/myuser/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes"
image_default_transport = "docker://"
runtime = "runc"
conmon_path = ["/usr/libexec/podman/conmon", "/usr/libexec/crio/conmon", "/usr/local/lib/podman/conmon", "/usr/local/libexec/crio/conmon", "/usr/bin/conmon", "/usr/sbin/conmon", "/usr/lib/crio/bin/conmon"]
conmon_env_vars = ["PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/sbin:/sbin:/bin"]
cgroup_manager = "cgroupfs"
init_path = "/usr/libexec/podman/catatonit"
static_dir = "/storage/home/myuser/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod"
tmp_dir = "/run/user/4603/libpod/tmp"
max_long_size = -1
no_pivot_root = false
cni_config_dir = "/etc/cni/net.d"
cni_plugin_dir = ["/usr/libexec/cni", "/usr/lib/cni", "/usr/local/lib/cni", "/opt/cni/bin"]
infra_image = "k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.1"
infra_command = "/pause"
enable_port_reservation = true
label = true
network_cmd_path = ""
num_locks = 2048
events_logger = "journald"
EventsLogfilePath = ""

[runtimes]
    runc = ["/usr/bin/runc", "/usr/sbin/runc", "/usr/local/bin/runc", "/usr/local/sbin/runc", "/sbin/runc", "/bin/runc", "/usr/lib/cri-o-runc/sbin/runc"]

$ cat /etc/subuid
. . . . . . 
myuser:493216:65536
. . . . . .

$ cat /etc/subgid
. . . . . . 
myuser:493216:65536
. . . . . .
```

comments.body:
Fixed the volume path in libpod.conf to /var/tmp/myuser/containers/storage/volumes.
But there seems a little problem while using podman on SAN or NFS environment
The NFS issue is known, we'd like to get it fixed in the semi-near future (sometime in the next year)
Yes, it seems that many problems related to NFS(or SAN) and file permissions might be solved by using the path outside of external storage(ex. root directory)
